---
title: "Deploying Content Federated Connectors"
---

---
title: "Deploying Content Federated Connectors"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Content-Federation-Services/uqx1742412928336"
section: "Content Federation Services"
---

Content Federated connectors are deployed as part of released projects created within Automate. Content Federated connectors are configured in the last step of the deployment process.

Before a project can be deployed, you need to release the project. For more information on how to release a project in Automate, see Releasing Projects in the Hyland Automate Administration documentation.
To deploy a project that contains a Content Federated connector:

1.  Log in to the Automate Administration App.
2.  In the left pane, select Devops > Project Releases. The Project Releases page is displayed.
3.  Click the Options button (![Options button](data:image/gif;base64,)) next to the project that includes a Content Federated connector.
4.  Click Deploy. The Application Name page of the Deploy Application wizard is displayed.Note: For more information on the available options and fields in the Deploy Application wizard, see Deploying Projects in the Hyland Automate Administration documentation.
5.  Configure the following fields:
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Application Name | Enter a unique name for the project. |
| Project Release | Select the release of the project you want to deploy. |
| Environments | Select the environment in which the application is to be deployed. |

6.  To enable development of custom UI applications, select Enable local development.
7.  Click Next. The Validation page of the wizard is displayed.
8.  Ensure the validation of the project is successful.
9.  Click Next. The Admin Access page of the wizard is displayed.
10.  To grant administrative access to the project, do one of the following:
- To grant administrative access to individual users, select the users by entering their usernames in the Admin users field.
- To grant administrative access to user groups, select the user groups by enter their user group names in the Admin groups field.
11.  Click Next. The Manager Access page of the wizard is displayed.
12.  To grant manager access to the project, do one of the following:
- To grant manager access to individual users, select the users by entering their usernames in the Manager users field.
- To grant manager access to user groups, select the user groups by entering their user group names in the Manager groups field.
13.  Click Next. The User Access page of the wizard is displayed.
14.  To grant user-level access to the project, do one of the following:
- To grant user-level access to individual users, select the users by entering their usernames in the Users field.
- To grant user-level access to user groups, select the user groups by entering their user group names in the User groups field.
15.  Click Next. The Connectors page of the wizard is displayed.
16.  Expand the editable fields by clicking Content Connector.
17.  Configure fields based on whether the Content Federated connector in the project being deployed is connecting on behalf of an end user or through a service user. For more information, see Methods of User Configuration.
- If the project contains a Content Federated connector that is connecting on behalf of (OBO) an end user, configure the following fields:
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CONNECTOR_CONNECTION_IDENTIFIER | ID property associated with the CFS Connection in the Content Federation Admin Portal |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_ID | Not required for OBO |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_SECRET | Not required for OBO |

- If the project contains a Content Federated connector that is connecting through a service user (SU), configure the following fields:
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CFS_CONNECTION_IDENTIFIER | ID property associated with the CFS Connection in the Content Federation Admin Portal |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_ID | Identifier of the registered external application in the CIC |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_SECRET | Secret associated with the registered external application in the CIC |

18.  Click Deploy. The project is deployed and made available to users.
